Name: Crobat
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent’s hand. If Girafarig and the Defending Pokémon don’t have the same number of Energy cards attached to them, this attack’s base damage is 10 instead of 40.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Zubat, Golbat, and Crobat on your Bench.

Name: Granbull
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
You may discard up to 2 cards from your hand. If you do, draw that many cards.

Name: Heracross
Effects: If there are 4 or more damage counters on Heracross, this attack’s base damage is 50 instead of 30.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Houndour
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Houndour.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy card attached to Houndour.

Name: Igglybuff
Effects: Choose 1 of your Pokémon with an Energy card attached to it. Remove 1 damage counter from that Pokémon.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from Jigglypuff.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Kadabra
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance.

Name: Kakuna
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: All damage by attacks to Kakuna is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Ledyba
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Ledyba by attacks is reduced by 20.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Ledyba
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Machop
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Magikarp, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

If the Defending Pokémon has any basic Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and move it to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, ignore this effect.

Name: Mantine
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads
Don’t apply Resistance.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent’s hand. If he or she has any Energy cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Put a damage counter on that Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has a damage counter on it. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance.

Name: Natu
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of either player’s deck and rearrange them as you like.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card named Nidoran Male or Nidoran Female and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy cards attached to Pikachu. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

Name: Pineco
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Pineco
Effects: Pineco does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If you get 1 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If you get 2 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. If you get 3 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 60 more damage.

Name: Rattata
Effects: Search your deck for a Baby Pokémon or Basic Pokémon card and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Rhyhorn.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

Name: Sandslash
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose up to 2 of them. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If you get at least 1 heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Seel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Seel
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Shuckle
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: All damage done by attacks to Shuckle is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, switch Skarmory with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Slugma
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy card attached to Slugma in order to use this attack. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Slugma
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Snorlax
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Snorlax is your Active Pokémon, you may remove 1 damage counter from Snorlax. Snorlax is now Asleep. This power can’t be used if Snorlax is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, until the end of your opponent’s next turn the Defending Pokémon can’t attack or retreat.

Name: Stantler
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent’s hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to the Defending Pokémon (don’t apply Weakness or Resistance). If tails and your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Staryu
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Staryu for each Energy attached to it. If it has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Sunflora
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Sunkern
Effects: If there are any damage counters on Sunkern, remove 1 of them.

Name: Swinub
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Swinub
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Teddiursa.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If not, this attack does 40 damage.

Name: Venomoth
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. If a Pokémon has fewer damage counters than the number of heads, remove all of them.
Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition (Asleep, Burned, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned). The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

Name: Venonat
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.
Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Yanma.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

Name: Zubat
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Ancient Ruins
Description: Once during each player’s turn (before he or she attacks), if he or she has not played a Supporter card, that player may reveal his or her hand to his or her opponent. If that player reveals his or her hand and there is no Supporter card there, that player draws a card.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Name: Relic Hunter
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Supporter and/or Stadium cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Apricorn Maker
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards with Ball in their names, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Crystal Shard
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon’s type (color) is [C]. If that Pokémon attacks, discard this card at the end of the turn.
Tool-Rule: Attach Crystal Shard to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Desert Shaman
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck and draw up to 4 cards. Your opponent does the same.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Fast Ball
Description: Reveal cards from your deck until you reveal an Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other revealed cards into your deck. (If you don’t reveal an Evolution card, shuffle all the revealed cards back into your deck.)

Name: Fisherman
Description: Choose 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile (if there are fewer basic Energy cards than choose, take all of them), show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Friend Ball
Description: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Search your deck for a Baby Pokémon, Basic Pokémon, or Evolution card of the same type (color), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Hyper Potion
Description: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Discard 1 or 2 basic Energy cards attached to that Pokémon. If you discarded 1 Energy card, remove up to 3 damage counters from that Pokémon. If you discarded 2 Energy cards, remove up to 5 damage counters from that Pokémon.

Name: Lure Ball
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, choose an Evolution card from your discard pile, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

Name: Miracle Sphere ⍺
Effects: If the Pokémon using this attack has [R] and [L] basic Energy cards attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If the Pokémon using this attack has [R] and [F] basic Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage and remove 3 damage counters from the Pokémon that Miracle Sphere ⍺ is attached to.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Evolved {R}, {L}, or {F} Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Miracle Sphere ⍺.

Name: Miracle Sphere β
Effects: If the Pokémon using this attack has [R] and [W] basic Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If the Pokémon using this attack has [W] and [P] basic Energy cards attached to it, choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon, if any, and your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Evolved {R}, {W}, or {P} Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Miracle Sphere β.

Name: Miracle Sphere 𝛾
Effects: If the Pokémon using this attack has [G] and [L] basic Energy cards attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. If the Pokémon using this attack has [W] and [L] basic Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage and lets you put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Evolved {G}, {W}, or {L} Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Miracle Sphere γ.

Name: Mirage Stadium
Description: Whenever a player tries to retreat a Pokémon during his or her turn, that player flips a coin. If heads, that player retreats that Pokémon (and discards Energy normally). If tails, that Pokémon can’t retreat this turn (the player doesn’t discard any Energy).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Name: Mystery Plate ⍺
Effects: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If your opponent has only 1 Prize, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate ⍺.

Name: Mystery Plate β
Effects: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, draw 3 cards. If your opponent has only 1 Prize, choose 2 Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon (1 if there is only 1). Your opponent shuffles those cards into his or her deck.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate β.

Name: Mystery Plate 𝛾
Effects: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, shuffle your hand into your deck and then draw 6 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2 Prizes, choose 1 of your opponent’s Evolved Pokémon. Your opponent puts the top card on that Evolved Pokémon on the bottom of his or her deck. (This counts as devolving that Pokémon.)
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate 𝛾.

Name: Mystery Plate δ
Effects: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If your opponent has exactly 2 Prizes, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate δ.

Name: Mystery Zone
Description: Once during each player’s turn (before he or she attacks), if that player has an Evolution card in his or her hand, he or she may search his or her deck for a basic Energy card, show it to his or her opponent, and put it into his or her hand. Then that player chooses an Evolution card from his or her hand, shows it to his or her opponent, and puts it into his or her deck. That player shuffles his or her deck afterward.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Name: Oracle
Description: Choose 2 cards from your deck and shuffle the rest of your deck. Put the chosen cards on top of your deck in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Star Piece
Description: At any time between turns, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is Benched and has 2 or more damage counters on it, search your deck for an Evolution card that Pokémon evolves into and put it on top of that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, discard Star Piece.
Tool-Rule: Attach Star Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Underground Expedition
Description: Look at the bottom 4 cards of your deck. Put 2 of those cards into your hand, and then return the remaining cards to the bottom of your deck in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Underground Lake
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may put an Omanyte or a Kabuto card from his or her discard pile onto his or her Bench. (Cards put on the Bench this way are considered Basic Pokémon.)
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Name: Bounce Energy
Description:
This card provides [C][C] Energy. You can attach this card to your Pokémon that has basic Energy cards attached to it.
When you play this card from your hand and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon, return a basic Energy card attached to that Pokémon to your hand.

Name: Cyclone Energy
Description:
This card provides [C] Energy.
When you play this card from your hand and attach it to your Active Pokémon, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

Name: Retro Energy
Description:
This card provides [C] Energy.
When you play this card from your hand and attach it to 1 of your Evolved Pokémon, you may remove up to 2 damage counters from that Pokémon and discard the top card from it. (This counts as devolving it.)

Name: Celebi
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [G], [W], or [P] basic Energy card from your hand to Celebi, Celebi’s type (color) becomes the same as that type of Energy until the end of the turn.
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Celebi and each Pokémon that’s the same type (color) as Celebi.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Charizard
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [R], [L], or [F] basic Energy card from your hand to Charizard, Charizard’s type (color) becomes the same as that type of Energy until the end of the turn.
Effects: Discard an Energy card attached to Charizard.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Crobat
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [G], [R], or [P] basic Energy card from your hand to Crobat, Crobat’s type (color) becomes the same as that type of Energy until the end of the turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Golem
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [G], [R], or [F] basic Energy card from your hand to Golem, Golem’s type (color) becomes the same as that type of Energy until the end of the turn.
Effects: Golem does 20 damage to itself. This attack also does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Ho-Oh
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [R], [W], or [L] basic Energy card from your hand to Ho-oh, Ho-oh’s type (color) becomes the same as that type of Energy until the end of the turn.
Effects: Discard an Energy card attached to Ho-oh and flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Kabutops
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [W], [L], or [F] basic Energy card from your hand to Kabutops, Kabutops’s type (color) becomes the same as that type of Energy until the end of the turn.
Effects: Remove a damage counter from Kabutops.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
